description: The American Mafia, also known as La Cosa Nostra, is an Italian-American organized crime society that emerged in the United States in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, originating from Sicilian Mafia roots. It operates through various 'families' across major U.S. cities, engaging in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. The organization is still active, with thousands of members and associates.

country_links.evidence_quote: La familia criminal Bonanno y la familia criminal de Búffalo tuvieron también influencia en varias facciones en Canadá incluyendo a la familia criminal Rizzuto y la familia criminal Cotroni , y la familia criminal Luppino y familia criminal Papalia , respectivamente.
country_links.context: The American Mafia has families with members and associates working in Canada, specifically the Bonanno and Buffalo families have influence over factions there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Meyer Lansky tejió una red de contactos en la industria de los casinos en Cuba durante los años 1930 mientras la mafia ya se había envuelto en la exportación de azúcar y ron cubano.
country_links.context: The American Mafia invested in casinos and operated in Cuba during the Batista regime.

country_links.evidence_quote: Se calcula que un estimado de 100 mafiosi de los Estados Unidos, Italia y Cuba asistieron a la reunión.
country_links.context: Mafiosi from Italy attended the Apalachin meeting.
